beats.activity: Wake up, make instant ramen, scroll Discord while eating breakfast
beats.reasoning: Jin is a night owl who skips morning classes; 10:15 AM is typical wake-up time. Energy was low yesterday (35/100), so a slow morning with ramen and Discord fits the pattern.

beats.activity: Walk to campus via U-District streets
beats.reasoning: Transit strike means no Light Rail; Jin walks from U-District home to campus. Generous 30 min buffer for foot traffic and no buses available.

beats.activity: Study Data Structures problem sets in Allen Library
beats.reasoning: Consistent with recent days: Jin spends mornings/midday in the library working on coursework. Mild 68F weather means AC is comfortable, not overkill like yesterday's heat.

beats.activity: Grab cold pho at the food court
beats.location_name: University of Washington Food Court (U-District, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Mild weather (68F) means pho is back on the menu after yesterday's heat-driven cold pho. Quick campus meal, consistent routine.

beats.activity: Continue studying Data Structures in Allen Library
beats.reasoning: Jin stays on campus through early afternoon to leverage library time; transit strike means walking home is expensive in energy/time, so batching work makes sense.

beats.activity: Walk to IMA for pickup basketball
beats.location_name: IMA (Intramural Complex, University of Washington, U-District)
beats.reasoning: Within campus, short walk. Transit strike is over by 4pm, but Jin prefers walking short distances on campus anyway.

beats.activity: Play pickup basketball at the Intramural Complex
beats.location_name: IMA (Intramural Complex, University of Washington, U-District)
beats.reasoning: Daily ritual for Jin. Clears his head, gets a sweat in, and the AC is a bonus on any day. Consistent with past three days.
Short walk from IMA to U-District ramen shop; Jin stops here almost every day after basketball. Transit strike is now over, but walking is fine for this short distance.

beats.activity: Eat warm ramen and decompress solo in corner booth
beats.reasoning: Post-basketball ritual. Jin eats alone, lets the warmth settle him, and resets before evening study session. Matches yesterday's pattern exactly.
Short walk from Taneda back to dorm. Transit is now operational, but Jin prefers walking this familiar neighborhood route.

beats.activity: Study Data Structures and other coursework at home and library
beats.reasoning: Evening study session. Jin is a night owl who studies until 2 AM; starting at home and potentially heading back to Allen later is typical. For now, early evening work at dorm.

beats.activity: Wind down: scroll Discord, energy drink, prepare for late-night library run
beats.reasoning: Jin is a night owl; winding down at home before the final push. Energy drink kicks in for the 2 AM library grind. Leisure beat ensures he's home when sleep begins.
6.5-hour sleep window ending before 06:00. Jin's low energy from yesterday (35/100) suggests he needs solid rest tonight before another day of campus life.

diary: Woke up late and made instant ramen—basically my entire food pyramid at this point. The transit strike meant walking everywhere today, which honestly wasn't terrible since the weather was nice. Spent most of the morning grinding Data Structures problem sets in Allen Library, grabbed cold pho at the food court, then headed to IMA for pickup basketball. That was the highlight—got some solid games in, felt good to move after hunching over code all morning. Hit up Taneda Ramen after to refuel, sat alone in a corner booth just decompressing with a warm bowl. The rest of the day blurred into more studying at home. My brain's pretty fried now, but I've got a late-night library run planned. There's something about the quiet of campus at night that makes the work feel less suffocating.
thought_process: The transit strike actually worked in my favor—walking to campus forced me outside and the weather was decent. I could've paid surge prices for a rideshare but I'm broke enough as is. Basketball was non-negotiable today; I needed to break up the studying before I completely burned out. I noticed my mood shifted after playing—less anxious about the problem sets. The solo ramen session was intentional; I needed quiet after the court. By evening I was running on fumes and an energy drink, but I know cramming tonight is necessary. There's a rhythm to my procrastination that I'm not proud of but it somehow works. The strike didn't really stress me since I'm already campus-adjacent in U-District.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
